title: Identifier cross-reference resolver (logged, reversible)
context: The same model appears across sources under different identifiers; the resolver merges them into one canonical entity with explicit, logged, reversible rules and per-source provenance, never silently overwriting conflicting values (PLAN M1, O3). Depends on the Cellosaurus+DepMap ingest (007). Binding cancer guardrails (PLAN section 7): open-access/aggregate/de-identified metadata only; no controlled-access (dbGaP/EGA) or patient-identifiable data; COSMIC/OncoKB reference-only (not ingested); provenance on every assertion; links only, no omics/sequence/image payloads re-hosted; no medical advice. No partner/requestor secured yet, so verifiedNeed is false.
objective: Implement an identifier cross-reference resolver that merges same-model records under explicit, logged, reversible rules while surfacing conflicts with per-source provenance.
acceptanceCriteria: Same model across sources merged into one canonical entity; rules explicit + logged; no silent merges; merges reversible.
Conflicting field values surfaced with per-source provenance, not overwritten.
output: A merged PR adding the identifier cross-reference resolver with a merge log and conflict surfacing.
